discrepancies.subject: LOAD-BEARING strategic divergence: PROMPT 199 corpus reframe vs PROMPT 198 AGGREGATE_72H plan
discrepancies.prompt_198_position: Q-198-1 A1: fire slot 153 3-step Zenodo cascade (PCF-2 v1.4 -> umbrella v2.2 -> picture-chain v1.20+) on 2026-05-12 PM JST as dependency root for half the admin window. AGGREGATE_72H Day-2.
discrepancies.prompt_199_position: What-to-table sec bullet 2: 'gently push back on continuing to deposit umbrella revisions as standalone Zenodo records (v2.0 -> v2.2 -> v2.3 forthcoming). These are provenance churn and they dilute the citation graph.' Plus what-to-table bullet 3: 'temptation to keep extending the cascade rather than crystallizing what's already proven.'
discrepancies.specific_operative_conflict: S153 step 2 = umbrella v2.2 Zenodo deposit. PROMPT 199 says umbrella deposits should pause / convert to bridge living-document.
discrepancies.substrate_prep_mitigation: Umbrella v2.2 substrate-prep already LANDED at bridge 887981b (slot 135) on 2026-05-09. Picture v1.20+ at b9aa881 (slot 136) on 2026-05-10. Aborting forfeits prepped work.
discrepancies.synthesis_recommendation_cli_agent: HYBRID -- honor 198 72h plan for already-prepped substrate (execute S153 cascade including umbrella v2.2 step); PAUSE umbrella v2.3 development indefinitely (or until Move 1 flagship is well underway); treat Moves 2-6 as post-72h-window priorities (no conflict); Move 1 = highest-value strategic priority post-72h.
discrepancies.verification_status: single-witness CLI-agent reconciliation; NOT synth-verified; dual-witness escalation OPTIONAL before AGGREGATE_72H Day-2 S153 fire
discrepancies.decision_deadline: 2026-05-12 PM JST (before AGGREGATE_72H Day-2 S153 fire)

discrepancies.subject: Move 1 readiness assertion vs current Frontier-A cascade-in-progress
discrepancies.prompt_199_assertion: What-to-table bullet 3: 'Programs A and B have enough closed results to support a flagship paper RIGHT NOW; Route F closure would strengthen Move 2 but isn't required for Moves 1, 3, 4, 5, 6.'
discrepancies.active_evidence_against: Frontier-A cascade (slots 188 LANDED + 189 LANDED + PROMPT 191 HALTED + Phase 0 191A pending + FIRE-A2'/A3'/A4' blocked) is structured around RESCOPED differentiation-check / Sakai-D6(1) PIII hierarchy construction. If those produce material findings, they would naturally fold into Move 1 flagship; current PROMPT 199 framing treats Frontier-A as separable from flagship readiness.
discrepancies.resolution_recommendation: Flagship paper Move 1 outline could fire NOW with current closed-result scope (PCF-1 / PCF-2 / Logarithmic Ladder / T2B / (4,2) / Finite-Depth Rigidity / Channel Theory + BT baseline + D2-NOTE); Frontier-A findings (when complete) could land as v2 of flagship or as Section X addendum. Need not gate Move 1 on Frontier-A completion.

discrepancies.subject: PROMPT 199 freeform consultation provenance vs SIARC structured-dispatch discipline
discrepancies.observation: PROMPT 199 arrived without CLI-drafted dispatch (no PROMPT file in tex/submitted/control center/prompt/199_*.txt). Operator initiated claude.ai freeform consultation; verdict pasted to CLI for absorption.
discrepancies.structural_implications: (a) No Q-191-5 self-check applied to the dispatch (no dispatch file existed); (b) No bibliographic pre-verification slot for any external identifiers (none cited beyond researcher names); (c) No structured question-numbering means absorption uses synth's natural sections rather than Q-199-N format; (d) Dual-witness escalation framework less crisp without structured dispatch as reference.
discrepancies.process_question: Should freeform consultations be brought into structured SIARC framework (operator dispatches CLI-drafted PROMPT first, then dispatches to claude.ai), OR is FREEFORM-CONSULT a legitimate dispatch class with its own absorption template?
discrepancies.candidate_memory: Freeform claude.ai consultations are valid dispatch class; absorb at bridge with FREEFORM-CONSULT provenance tag; load-bearing tension with structured prompts triggers operator-decision discrepancy entry
